fact: May 4 is exactly 124 days into the year, which means there are exactly 241 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, May 4 falls smack in the middle of spring migration madness - millions of butterflies are fluttering north right now, including monarchs that can fly 3,000 miles!
The sun rises around 6 AM and sets around 7:30 PM in most of North America on May 4, giving you almost 13.5 hours of daylight to play outside.
May 4 babies are Taurus zodiac signs, which means they're supposedly as stubborn as bulls but also love snacks and cozy blankets.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, May 4 marks the heart of autumn when leaves turn brilliant orange and red, the exact opposite of what's happening up north!
Tree sap is flowing like crazy around May 4 in cold regions - maple trees can produce up to 10 gallons of sap per day, which boils down to less than one quart of syrup!
The moon phase on May 4 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it's called the Flower Moon because so many plants are blooming everywhere.
May 4 sits exactly 46 days after the spring equinox and 77 days before the summer solstice, making it the perfect middle point of spring's growth explosion.
In many places, May 4 brings the first really warm weather that makes earthworms come up after rain, creating a slimy sidewalk obstacle course.
Dandelions are at their peak puffball stage around May 4 in temperate regions - one dandelion head contains about 2,000 seeds ready to fly away and annoy gardeners everywhere.
The average temperature on May 4 in the northern United States hovers around 65°F (18°C), which is scientifically proven to be perfect hoodie weather.
May 4 marks peak tadpole season in ponds across North America - millions of tiny black dots are swimming around, growing legs and losing their tails in the weirdest transformation ever.
